title: ulmus minor var. suberosa
intro: Ulmus minor var. suberosa, also known as Cork Elm or Smooth-leaved Elm, is a variety of the small-leaved elm tree native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ulmus minor var. suberosa include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.
